preface.p: Development of the backend for the LLVM compiler: github.com/bdpx/llvm-project.

section.title: LLVM backend limitations
section.p: Pre/post update addressing is not used.
Currently, only static PIE executables are supported by compiler and emulator.

section.title: MUSL port
section.p: limitations: currently, buildable only as a static lib.

section.title: Code density comparison
section.p: Here are results for SQLite 3.33.0 compiled with Clang 10.0.1 on FreeBSD 12.1 with -Os for various architectures:

where a32 is ARMv7-A in ARM mode, t32 is the same in thumb mode and a64 is ARMv8-A. The rest are self explanatory. The clear winner is ARM Thumb, but RISC-V does well indeed (with compressed instructions, without it's rather meh) It's the most space efficient 64 bit ISA for sure. i686 does a little worse (still the third most compact after RV32gc and T32) and the classic RISC instruction sets are just terrible. The clear loser is Z/Architecture (S390x).
The probably same SQLite 3.33.0 sqlite-chromium-version-3.33.0, compiled with the port for Clang 22.0 on Linux and for comparison x86-64 Clang 19 and gcc 14.
section.code: text data bss dec hex filename 838764 14888 1683 855335 d0d27 sqlite3.postrisc.O2-aligned-null.o 863884 14888 1683 880455 d6f47 sqlite3.postrisc.O2-aligned.o 790236 14888 1683 806807 c4f97 sqlite3.postrisc.O2-dense-null.o 818636 14888 1683 835207 cbe87 sqlite3.postrisc.O2-dense.o 823100 14888 1683 839671 ccff7 sqlite3.postrisc.Os-aligned-null.o 844284 14888 1683 860855 d22b7 sqlite3.postrisc.Os-aligned.o 774812 14888 1683 791383 c1357 sqlite3.postrisc.Os-dense-null.o 798828 14888 1683 815399 c7127 sqlite3.postrisc.Os-dense.o 749234 14952 1691 765877 bafb5 sqlite3.x86_64.O2.o 708074 14952 1691 724717 b0eed sqlite3.x86_64.Os.o
section.p: The results for are without using the post-update addressing modes (not implemented yet in the compiler) which may improve code density a bit. The main factor for code density is the possibility of returning inside the middle of an instruction bundle (dense calls). In common, code density for is more or less similar to MIPS, PowerPC, S-390, and RISC-V (without compression) and only slightly worse. This is surprising taking into account 128 registers, bundles, nops, etc. For O2 mode results are almost similar to clang-x86_64.

section.title: DOOM port
section.p: port for Doom-1: github.com/bdpx/postrisc_doom. Uses the MUSL standard library (as a static lib). Doom generic interface is implemented as additional system calls.

The emulator log doom-log.html with static/dynamic instruction statistic for Doom Shareware demo scene autoplay. First 3 demoscenes (around). Time: 341.022 seconds. Frames: 16321. Iinstructions per frame: 851263 (up to 8-bit indexed image not counting emulator scaling/mapping). Instructions per pixel: 13.301. Frames per second: 48.191.
